kept.text:
Crypto payment cards are still in the early stages of global adoption. Regulatory frameworks remain fragmented, and access continues to vary significantly across regions.

👉Against this backdrop, @KoloHub has established a notable presence operating in over 170 countries and serving more than 10 million users. This level of scale signals strong execution and a clear focus on usability.

👉KOLO Card: Key Benefits

🔥Global Accessibility
Available across a wide range of markets, enabling users to access crypto powered payments without geographic limitations.

🔥Multi-Asset Support
Seamlessly manage and spend major digital assets, including BTC, ETH, USDC, TON, and others, all within a single platform.

🔥All in One Functionality
Core financial actions like top-ups, conversions, and transfers, are fully integrated, reducing friction and simplifying the user experience.

🔥User Centric Design
A clean, intuitive interface built for everyday use, making crypto payments more approachable for both new and experienced users.

🔥Fast Onboarding
Streamlined account setup through mobile app and Telegram integration, enabling users to get started quickly.

As crypto cards continue to move toward mainstream adoption, platforms that prioritize accessibility, reliability, and simplicity will shape the category. Kolo is positioning itself in alignment with these principles, focusing on practical, real world usability.

#KOLO #DebitCard

kept.text:
Revolut injects another $64M into Mexico — 290,000 customers in just 9 weeks.

Revolut has injected another $64M into its Mexican banking subsidiary in April, lifting its total investment in the country to $167M. After launching full banking operations on 27 January 2026, the company already reports over 290,000 registered Mexican customers for Q1.

Deposits reached MXN 3,753M (~$200M), driven by competitive interest-bearing accounts. The credit portfolio grew by MXN 44M in low-risk Stage 1 assets, with more than 12,000 active credit card clients.

Asset quality remains pristine: the non-performing loan ratio (IMOR) is just 0.02%, with 99.98% of credit in Stage 1.

Liquidity and capital are equally strong — Liquidity Coverage Ratio at 1,531% and Capitalisation Index at 141.6%.

As expected in the heavy investment phase, Revolut Mexico posted a MXN 153M net loss in Q1, fully in line with plan.

CEO Juan Miguel Guerra Dávila said, "The response of Mexicans has exceeded our expectations. Clearly, there exists a great appetite for a banking application that offers everything in one place."

Looks like Revolut is executing a textbook challenger bank entry in Mexico — investing capital for growth, maintaining strong capital ratios, and seeing good customer uptake — despite the expected early losses. But still, it has a huge mountain to climb to become the dominant bank in the country. Let's see if momentul holds.

Nubank reported weeks ago that it had reached 15 million customers in Mexico.

Revolut has just disabled its freshly launched PayTo top-up functionality in Australia following an undisclosed security incident.

Customers received emails explaining that existing PayTo agreements to add money had been cancelled, and that new ones were blocked while Revolut investigates. Outgoing PayTo payments remain untouched, and the company insists no customer funds or personal data were compromised.

PayTo, Australia’s real-time account-to-account payment rail built on the New Payments Platform (NPP), went live for Revolut users around mid-March 2026. It promised easier recurring payments, better visibility, and stronger controls than old direct debits. Now it’s partially offline for inbound top-ups, with Revolut citing “proactive security measures” during the investigation.

Rather than risk exposure, Revolut moved decisively — a sensible move in an environment where payment fraud continues to evolve faster than most banks can react. Industry chatter points to issues involving third-party institutions connected via Revolut’s NPP provider, though details remain scarce.

For Revolut, this is an awkward early stumble in one of its key growth markets. Hope this incident will not affect Revolut's banking license in Australia.

Let's see how it goes.

quoted_tweet.text:
Revolut has launched a new advertising campaign in Spain starring former football player Luís Figo.

The campaign began with a large banner in Madrid's Glorieta de Bilbao, above a Santander Bank branch, featuring the slogan "Do it for your money - Switch to Revolut," a direct reference to his historic transfer team, reinterpreted to encourage switching banks.

The banner will remain visible until May 18th

In the video, Figo speaks about the importance of managing your money, making the best decisions, and switching banks.

The campaign will also launch in the Portuguese market, with Luís Figo wanting his compatriots to embrace change and take charge of their finances, just as he did.

"Choosing Luís Figo is no coincidence; he starred in the most famous transfer in recent Spanish sporting history, revolutionizing the transfer system of his time. Similarly, at Revolut, we want people to embrace change and break free from traditional banking practices," says Ignacio Zunzunegui, Head of Growth for Southern Europe at Revolut. 

 "This campaign uses humour and provocation to deliver a very clear message: switching to Revolut is, today, the smartest transfer you can make ."

Luís Figo emphasised that: "Revisiting this campaign with a humorous twist has been fun. Together with Revolut, we wanted to convey a simple but important message: changing banks isn't offside, it can actually be a spectacular goal."

Source: CRTL Magazine
#Spain #Portugal
